root_text.sn47.25:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn47.25:1.3: Atha kho aññataro brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn47.25:1.4: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn47.25:1.5: “ko nu kho, bho gotama, hetu, ko paccayo yena tathāgate parinibbute saddhammo na ciraṭṭhitiko hoti?
root_text.sn47.25:1.6: Ko pana, bho gotama, hetu, ko paccayo yena tathāgate parinibbute saddhammo ciraṭṭhitiko hotī”ti?
root_text.sn47.25:2.1: “Catunnaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, satipaṭṭhānānaṁ abhāvitattā abahulīkatattā tathāgate parinibbute saddhammo na ciraṭṭhitiko hoti.
root_text.sn47.25:2.2: Catunnañca kho, brāhmaṇa, satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā tathāgate parinibbute saddhammo ciraṭṭhitiko hoti.
root_text.sn47.25:3.2: Idha, brāhmaṇa, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.25:3.5: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.25:3.6: Imesaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, catunnaṁ satipaṭṭhānānaṁ abhāvitattā abahulīkatattā tathāgate parinibbute saddhammo na ciraṭṭhitiko hoti.
root_text.sn47.25:3.7: Imesañca kho, brāhmaṇa, catunnaṁ satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā tathāgate parinibbute saddhammo ciraṭṭhitiko hotī”ti.
root_text.sn47.25:4.1: Evaṁ vutte, so brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn47.25:4.3: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.sn47.25:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.sn47.25:1.3: Then a certain brahmin went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn47.25:1.4: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn47.25:1.5: “What is the cause, worthy Gotama, what is the reason why the true teaching does not last long after the final quenching of the Realized One?
translation_text.sn47.25:1.6: And what is the cause, what is the reason why the true teaching does last long after the final quenching of the Realized One?”
translation_text.sn47.25:2.1: “Brahmin, it’s because of not developing and cultivating the four kinds of mindfulness meditation that the true teaching doesn’t last long after the final quenching of the Realized One.
translation_text.sn47.25:2.2: It’s because of developing and cultivating the four kinds of mindfulness meditation that the true teaching does last long after the final quenching of the Realized One.
translation_text.sn47.25:3.2: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.25:3.3: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.25:3.5: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.25:3.6: It’s because of not developing and cultivating these four kinds of mindfulness meditation that the true teaching doesn’t last long after the final quenching of the Realized One.
translation_text.sn47.25:3.7: It’s because of developing and cultivating these four kinds of mindfulness meditation that the true teaching does last long after the final quenching of the Realized One.”
translation_text.sn47.25:4.1: When he said this, the brahmin said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn47.25:4.3: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
